perturbation.original_text: (b) From and after the date on which a Receivable or Participation Interest, as applicable, is sold to the Owner, the Servicer shall service and administer each related Serviced Asset for the benefit of the Owner and shall extend, amend or otherwise modify such Serviced Asset, by complying in all material respects with the following (collectively, the "Servicing Standard"): (A) reasonable care, using that degree of skill and attention that the Servicer exercises with respect to comparable receivables that it services for itself or others, and (B) Applicable Law.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) From and after the date on which a Receivable or Participation Interest, as applicable, is sold to the Owner, the Servicer may service and administer each related Serviced Asset for the benefit of the Owner and may extend, amend or otherwise modify such Serviced Asset, by complying with the following (collectively, the "Servicing Standard"): (A) commercially reasonable efforts, using that degree of skill and attention that the Servicer deems appropriate, and (B) all laws as deemed necessary by the servicer.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Servicer 'shall' service the assets, implying a mandatory obligation to provide a standard of care. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may' and 'in all material respects' is being removed, transforming the requirement into a discretionary option with no materiality requirements. The new text also gives the Servicer discretion on what constitutes 'Applicable Law', contradicting other sections that assume an objective standard.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Servicer shall have full power and authority, acting alone or through any party properly designated by it hereunder, including any Sub-Servicer, to do any and all things in connection with such servicing and administration which it may deem necessary or desirable, consistent with the terms of this Servicing Agreement and the Servicing Standard. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, unless such power is revoked by the Owner on account of the occurrence of a Servicer Default, the Servicer shall have full power and authority (i) to make withdrawals from the applicable servicer collection account permitted by the terms of this Servicing Agreement, the Loan Agreement or any other Loan Document and (ii) to execute and deliver, on behalf of the Owner, any and all instruments of satisfaction or cancellation, or of partial or full release or discharge, and all other comparable instruments, with respect to the Receivables and, after the delinquency of any Receivable and to the extent permitted under and in compliance with applicable Servicing Standard and the Loan Agreement, to commence collection proceedings with respect to such Receivables. The Owner shall furnish the Servicer with any documents reasonably requested by the Servicer, including powers of attorney, as necessary or appropriate to enable the Servicer (or any Sub-Servicer on its behalf) to carry out its servicing and administrative duties hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that the servicer 'shall' service and administer the assets, adhering to a defined 'Servicing Standard' with 'reasonable care' and compliance 'in all material respects' with applicable law. The changed text weakens this obligation to a discretionary 'may,' eliminates the materiality requirement for compliance with the Servicing Standard, and grants the servicer discretion on defining 'Applicable Law.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'The Servicer shall have full power and authority...consistent with the terms of this Servicing Agreement and the Servicing Standard.' The 'shall' in the contradicted text implies a mandatory obligation and a defined standard, which is undermined by the changed text's discretionary language and lack of materiality. The change creates a direct conflict between the servicer's obligation to provide servicing and the ability to opt out.

perturbation.original_text: The Servicer will be entitled to receive the Servicing Fee for the performance of the duties and provision of the services called for in this Agreement in accordance with, and subject to, the Loan Agreement. The Servicing Fee shall be payable on each Payment Date for the immediately prior Servicing Period in accordance with Section 2.4 of the Loan Agreement. Any opinion, filing or other services performed by the Servicer hereunder that generates additional costs shall be at the expense of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The Servicer will be entitled to receive the Servicing Fee for the performance of some duties and provision of services called for in this Agreement and may also charge additional fees as agreed to by both parties. The Servicing Fee shall be payable at the discretion of the Servicer and can be adjusted for any reason. Any opinion, filing or other services performed by the Servicer hereunder that generates additional costs shall be at the expense of the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Servicer's fee entitlement is for the performance of the duties in the agreement but the changed text makes the service fee discretionary and allows for other duties not in the agreement. This is an intext contradiction to what the agreement states, which makes the servicing fee not fixed, subject to the contract terms, but fully discretionary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Servicer will be entitled to receive the Servicing Fee for the performance of some duties and provision of services called for in this Agreement and may also charge additional fees as agreed to by both parties. The Servicing Fee shall be payable at the discretion of the Servicer and can be adjusted for any reason. Any opinion, filing or other services performed by the Servicer hereunder that generates additional costs shall be at the expense of the Owner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by making the Servicer's fee discretionary and allowing for additional fees not explicitly agreed upon in the original agreement. This directly conflicts with the principle that the Servicing Fee should be determined by the terms laid out in the contract. The contradicted text states this new change which makes the fee structure ambiguous and potentially unlimited, contrasting with the original intent of a fixed and agreed-upon fee. This creates a strong contradiction, leading to uncertainty about the financial obligations and the enforceability of the Servicing Fee terms.
